# 1. Naming & Casing (naming)

**Impact:** CRITICAL

**Description:** Consistent naming and casing conventions are foundational to code readability. Inconsistent naming creates confusion, slows down code reviews, and makes maintenance harder.

## 2. Code Layout & Formatting (layout)

**Impact:** HIGH

**Description:** Good layout uses formatting to emphasize the structure of your code and to make the code easier to read. Consistent formatting allows readers to focus on content, not layout.

## 3. Code Patterns (code)

**Impact:** HIGH

**Description:** Coding patterns for variables, comments, namespaces, conditionals, strings, exceptions, operators, type conversions, DateTime handling, and static usage. Following these patterns ensures clean, maintainable, and testable code.

## 4. Unit Testing (testing)

**Impact:** HIGH

**Description:** Unit testing best practices including test naming, AAA pattern, code coverage, and Blazor testing. Well-written tests serve as executable documentation and protect against regressions.

## 5. Blazor Conventions (blazor)

**Impact:** MEDIUM-HIGH

**Description:** Blazor component conventions for naming, file structure, JavaScript interop, CSS isolation, and performance optimization. Following these patterns ensures maintainable and performant Blazor applications.
